items.title: Ripple to acquire Australian financial services license as APAC payments volume doubles
items.summary: The company plans to obtain the license through the acquisition of BC Payments Australia, expanding its regulated footprint to over 75 licenses worldwide.

items.title: Bitcoin steady above $70,000 as IEA proposes largest-ever oil reserve release
items.summary: The largest cryptocurrency gained 7% from Monday's lows as energy price fears eased, with Asian equities rising 1.8% and Brent crude dropping below $90 for the first time since the war began.

items.title: XRP Ledger transactions jump to 2.7M as price stays rangebound
items.summary: Traders are watching whether support near $1.34 holds after repeated rejection near $1.44.

items.title: Bitcoin traders bet on a rally above $80,000
items.summary: Many traders expect bitcoin to recover toward the $80K level between June and September, Derive said.

items.title: Democrats Introduce Bill to Ban Prediction Market Contracts on War and Death
items.summary: The DEATH BETS Act would prohibit CFTC-registered exchanges from listing contracts tied to terrorism, assassination, war, or death.

items.title: Dogecoin zooms as Elon Musk announces X Money launch date for April
items.summary: The payments app offers 6% yield on balances and peer-to-peer transfers inside X, competing directly with the same demand stablecoin products are targeting. No mention of dogecoin or any crypto functionality was included.

items.title: Crypto is just finance with new plumbing: Australia’s ASIC fintech chief
items.summary: New regulatory frameworks weren’t needed when financial infrastructure shifted from paper to electronic records, so it isn't needed for blockchain either, argues ASIC’s Rhys Bollen.

items.title: Ethereum's on fire with record activity, but ether price and blockchain fees lag
items.summary: Capital outflows, even as activity surges across Ethereum’s ecosystem, highlight the growing disconnect between usage growth and ETH’s market performance, a CryptoQuant report shows.

items.title: Nvidia's Huang: AI will boost jobs as it needs trillions in infrastructure
items.summary: Nvidia founder Jensen Huang says AI will create countless jobs as buildout for the tech has only just started and will require many more workers.

items.title: Ripple Seeks Australian Financial License to 'Scale' Payments Across the Region
items.summary: If approved, the license would allow Ripple to run services and bring its infrastructure into the country’s financial system.

items.title: Bitcoin is back in ‘FOMO territory’ after crossing $70K: Santiment

items.title: Bitcoin permabull Arthur Hayes says he wouldn't bet $1 on BTC right now
items.summary: BitMEX co-founder Arthur Hayes said he will start buying Bitcoin when the US Federal Reserve eases monetary policy and starts printing money amid rising tensions in the Middle East.

items.title: China Plays the Long Game in AI While US Chases Superintelligence: Brookings
items.summary: A Brookings report says China’s AI strategy prioritizes efficiency, open-source adoption, and embedding AI into real-world systems.

items.title: Ripple targets April for Australian financial license via acquisition
items.summary: Ripple APAC managing director Fiona Murray said there was sufficient institutional interest in Australia to warrant the costs of acquiring an Australian Financial Services License.

items.summary: Experts warn that encrypted chats could face a “harvest now, decrypt later” risk as quantum computing advances.

items.title: DeFi lending platform Aave sees a rare $27 million liquidations after a price glitch
items.summary: The blockchain data flagged shows a spike in liquidations over the past 24 hours. Some observers believe the event may have been linked to a price update in an risk-oracle system that Aave uses to determine the value of collateral.

items.title: Kalshi suffers court loss in Ohio over sports betting lawsuit
items.summary: The prediction markets platform argued for an injunction against Ohio authorities, claiming that federal commodities laws superseded state laws on sport event contracts.

items.title: Ether funding rate flips negative: Are ETH bears back in control?

items.summary: BullshitBench tests whether AI models can detect nonsensical questions—or if they'll confidently answer them anyway. The results are dire.
